qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SQL injection in Presto/Trino _partition_query via latest_sub_partition with broken validation
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes SQL injection through unsanitized string interpolation in PrestoBaseEngineSpec._partition_query with a dead-code validation check. The advisory describes improper authorization where low-privileged users bypass data access controls by overwriting the SQL query of an existing dataset. These are different root causes: SQL injection via broken field validation vs. missing authorization check on dataset SQL modification.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory describes an improper authorization vulnerability (CWE-863) where low-privileged users bypass data access controls by overwriting the SQL query of an existing dataset in Apache Superset. The presto_partition_sqli finding describes SQL injection via unsanitized string interpolation in PrestoBaseEngineSpec._partition_query with a dead-code validation bug — a different vulnerability class and root cause.
evidence: Advisory root cause: missing authorization check when overwriting existing dataset SQL queries allows bypass of data access controls (CWE-863).
presto_partition_sqli root cause: unsanitized f-string interpolation in _partition_query with dead-code validation from chained-comparison bug — SQL injection, not authorization bypass.
